architecture_notes: A100-SXM4-80GB (Ampere): 108 SMs, Peak BF16/FP16 312 TFLOPS, Peak HBM BW 2.0 TB/s, ridge point 156 FLOP/byte. warp_cycles_per_instruction is UNAVAILABLE on this profiling run (all null); eligible_cycles_pct used as substitute latency-bound indicator. Tensor Core minimum tile: 16x16x16 for BF16/FP16 on Ampere. All ampere_sgemm_* kernels are FP32 SIMT path — Tensor Cores are NOT engaged.
optimization_ceiling: Top 3 operators account for 70.7% of attributed time. 77.4% of attributed GPU time (aten::mm group, 193 operators) runs on the FP32 SIMT path with zero Tensor Core utilization. A dtype cast to BF16 or FP16 for all linear projections is the single highest-impact optimization available: it routes all GEMM kernels to the Tensor Core path, which on A100 delivers 16x higher peak throughput (312 TFLOPS BF16 vs 19.5 TFLOPS FP32). The attention operator (4.6%) has severe register pressure (168 regs/thread, 24M spill bytes) suppressing occupancy to 6.2%; BF16 attention (Flash Attention 2) would both save memory bandwidth and reduce register pressure. Potential speedup from dtype-only change: 3-5x on GEMM-dominated workloads at this batch/seqlen.

warnings.deduplication_note: Layer deduplication was active: metrics for modules_1 through modules_10 were propagated from modules_0. Per-layer operators (kernel_count=1) represent single deduplicated measurements. The aggregated operators (aten::mm_0, aten::addmm_0, aten::_efficient_attention_forward_0) accumulate all invocations across layers and are the authoritative time figures for optimization prioritization.
warnings.unattributed_note: 240 kernels (12.6% of 1898 total) could not be attributed. This is within expected range for inductor-compiled models without --correlation-pass. Primary unattributed kernel: triton_per_fused_native_layer_norm_0 (layer norm Triton kernels). Attribution confidence is medium for NVTX-matched operators and does not affect GEMM operator classification (which uses high-confidence torch.profiler correlation data).
